---
title: "data viz course ggplot2 + sf"
date: today
format:
html:
self-contained: true
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
code-fold: show
code-tools: true
theme: cosmo
fig-width: 10
fig-height: 6
df-print: paged
execute:
warning: false
message: false
---
```{r}
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
#  install.packages(c("rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata"))
library(rnaturalearth)
library(dplyr)
```

# 1. Vector data: countries as sf polygons
```{r}
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
head(world, n=2)        # note the geometry column
st_crs(world)      # the CRS travels with the data
```

# 2. Plain map — geom_sf reads the geometry column automatically

```{r}
ggplot(world) + geom_sf()
```

# 3. Choropleth — same grammar as any other ggplot

```{r}
ggplot(world) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = pop_est / 1e6)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Population (M)", trans = "log10") +
  theme_minimal()
```

# 4. Reproject — the projection IS a layer (coord_sf)

```{r}
ggplot(world) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = pop_est / 1e6)) +
  coord_sf(crs = "+proj=eqearth") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(trans = "log10") +
  theme_minimal()
```

# 5. Layer points on top — proportional symbols

```{r}
capitals <- ne_download(
  scale = "medium",
  type  = "populated_places",
  returnclass = "sf"
)

ggplot(world) +
  geom_sf(fill = "grey92", colour = "white") +
  geom_sf(
  data = capitals,
  aes(
    size = POP_MAX),
    alpha = 0.5,
    colour = "firebrick"
  ) +
  coord_sf(crs = "+proj=eqearth") +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 8) +
  theme_void()
```
